generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance ManufacturingSystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensing component collecting contextual data traces from the manufacturing system's operational environment, including component states and interface interactions. Part of the Digital Shadow Reference Model's one-way data flow from physical to digital layers."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ManufacturingSystemContextualDataTransmitted
]

instance PhysicalSystemDataCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous data acquisition system integrating sensor readings, PLC telemetry, and embedded system logs from the physical manufacturing system. Operates via industrial communication protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) with event-driven or time-triggered transmission."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PhysicalSystemHeterogeneousDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance ManufacturingSystemContextualDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ManufacturingSystemSensor
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces representing the physical system's state, including component interactions and interface dynamics. Collected via automated one-way communication as defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model."
]

instance PhysicalSystemHeterogeneousDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PhysicalSystemDataCollector
    base:desc "Disparate data streams from sensors, PLCs, and embedded systems normalized and filtered to ensure consistency before aggregation. Includes operational logs, telemetry, and real-time parameter updates."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model defining system decomposition into components, interfaces, and compositions across requirements, functional, and logical layers. Utilizes Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) for component-connector perspectives."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchainEnabler, ModelIntegrationEnabler
]

instance AADLComponentBasedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Architecture Analysis & Design Language (AADL) model defining component-based structure with explicit interfaces and composition rules. Supports hierarchical decomposition of the manufacturing system's physical and logical architecture."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLToolchainEnabler
]

instance MontiArcBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc model incorporating automata-based behavioral specifications for dynamic system interactions. Extends component-connector architecture with formal state transition definitions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcToolchainEnabler
]

instance SimulinkDynamicSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed behavioral and control logic simulation. Integrates with SysML and ADLs for cross-tool consistency in dynamic system analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolchainEnabler, ControlLogicEnabler
]

instance ManufacturingSystemContextualData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed contextual data traces abstracted from raw sensor/PLC inputs. Includes normalized time-series data, event logs, and interface state records for DT analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ManufacturingSystemContextualDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance HeterogeneousPhysicalSystemData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated heterogeneous data from disparate sources (sensors, PLCs, embedded systems) with validated quality metrics. Serves as input for cross-layer model integration."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PhysicalSystemHeterogeneousDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML toolchain enabling multi-layered abstraction modeling (requirements, functional, logical, product layers) with Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) for component-connector perspectives."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelingService, StakeholderIntegrationService
]

instance AADLToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL-based toolchain for component-based system decomposition with explicit interface definitions and composition rules. Supports hierarchical architecture modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables AADLArchitectureService
]

instance MontiArcToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework providing automata-based behavioral modeling extensions to component-connector architectures. Enables formal state machine integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink platform for detailed dynamic system simulation and control logic implementation. Bridges behavioral models with analytical requirements."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicSimulationService
]

instance ModelIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cross-tool integration mechanism ensuring consistency between SysML, ADLs, and simulation models. Manages heterogeneous model relationships across abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelConsistencyService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data preprocessing pipeline for normalization, filtering, and validation of contextual traces before aggregation. Ensures quality assurance for DT inputs."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataQualityService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous data integration framework combining sensor/PLC streams into unified digital representations. Aligns with MBSE methodologies for architectural consistency."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataUnificationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time and batch visualization platform leveraging SysML/ADL models and simulation outputs. Presents component interactions, interface states, and behavioral dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation-driven analytics engine performing predictive modeling, scenario analysis, and optimization using Simulink and other simulation tools."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveForecastingService
]

instance ControlLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Active control loop implementation using state machines, automata, or code-based logic to influence physical system behavior. Extends beyond passive Digital Shadow capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutonomousControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing multi-layered system modeling using SysML across requirements, functional, logical, and product architectures. Supports component-connector perspectives via IBDs."
    DTDFVocab:provides ManufacturingSystemArchitectureInsight, FunctionalDecompositionInsight
]

]

]

]

instance DataQualityService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance PredictiveForecastingService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

instance ModelConsistencyService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service maintaining consistency across heterogeneous models (SysML, ADLs, simulations) through cross-tool integration mechanisms."

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT) constellation** is implicitly defined through the **orchestration of heterogeneous modeling tools and methodologies** described in Section II.A. The document emphasizes a **multi-layered, multi-perspective approach** to system modeling, including **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**, which must be integrated into a cohesive DT system. Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  1. **Architecture Patterns**: The use of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML IBDs** to define **component & connector perspectives**, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. MontiArc, for example, extends this with **state machines** for behavioral modeling.
  2. **Tool Integration**: The DT constellation must integrate **diverse modeling notations and tools**, such as **SysML (for structural/logical modeling)**, **MathWorks Simulink (for behavioral/simulation modeling)**, and **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for implementation. This requires **interoperability frameworks** to bridge these tools, though no specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST) are mentioned.
  3. **Resource Management**: The document implies **stakeholder-specific perspectives** must be harmonized, suggesting a **resource-aware orchestration** to balance computational load, data consistency, and real-time constraints across layers. The **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) further suggests a **data-centric coordination** where aggregated contextual traces are processed by active DT components.
  4. **System-Wide Coordination**: While not explicitly detailed, the contrast between **digital shadows (passive, one-way data flow)** and **DTs (active, bidirectional interaction)** implies the constellation must support **event-driven or service-oriented coordination** to enable real-time adjustments between physical and digital domains. The lack of specific latency requirements or synchronization frequencies (e.g., millisecond-level updates) suggests this is domain-agnostic but dependent on the underlying MBSE methodology."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity and validity considerations** through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) validation and verification (V&V) processes**, though specific methods are not detailed in the provided document. Key aspects include:
  - **Model fidelity requirements** derived from **abstraction layers** (e.g., functional vs. technical) and **stakeholder-specific perspectives**, ensuring alignment with physical system behavior.
  - **Uncertainty sources** arise from **data abstraction** in digital shadows and **model-tool heterogeneity**, necessitating **quantification mechanisms** (e.g., statistical analysis of sensor data or simulation discrepancies).
  - **Error detection and correction** may involve **cross-model validation** (e.g., comparing SysML IBDs with Simulink simulations) and **automated consistency checks** across tools like **ACME or MontiArc**.
  - **Quality assurance** is implied through **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) but lacks explicit mention of **formal verification protocols** or **real-time error correction frameworks**. The document emphasizes the need for **active DT components** to process and interpret data, suggesting **runtime validation** as a critical but unspecified process."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical network infrastructure for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. However, the broader context of **MBSE and system modeling** implies reliance on **industrial-grade communication protocols** (e.g., OPC UA for interoperability, MQTT for lightweight IoT messaging) to facilitate data exchange between physical and digital layers. The **network topology** and **architecture** are likely **hybrid**, combining **edge computing nodes** (for low-latency local processing) and **centralized cloud/on-premise servers** (for storage and analytics). While **bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, the document emphasizes the need for **real-time or near-real-time data flows** (e.g., for digital shadows). **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** may include **failover protocols, data replication, or fault-tolerant architectures**, though specifics are absent. **Security protocols** for network communication are implied but not specified; however, industrial DTs typically employ **encryption (TLS/SSL), authentication (OAuth, certificates), and access control** to protect data integrity and confidentiality."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The deployment infrastructure for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly described in the document. However, the **MBSE context** suggests support for **multi-tiered deployment models**, including **cloud-based** (for scalability and global access), **edge computing** (for low-latency processing near physical assets), and **on-premise** (for proprietary or high-security environments). **Computational and storage requirements** are inferred to be **highly variable**, depending on the system’s complexity (e.g., real-time simulations may demand **GPU-accelerated servers** or **distributed computing clusters**). **Scalability mechanisms** likely involve **elastic resource allocation** (e.g., Kubernetes, serverless architectures) and **modular microservices** to handle dynamic workloads. **Availability and reliability assurance** may rely on **redundant infrastructure, load balancing, and automated failover**, though no specific technologies (e.g., AWS, Azure, or private cloud solutions) are mentioned. The document does not specify whether **hybrid deployment models** (combining cloud/edge/on-premise) are supported, but the integration of **heterogeneous tools** (e.g., Simulink, SysML) implies flexibility in deployment strategies."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document highlights **horizontal integration** as a critical challenge due to the **heterogeneous nature of Digital Twin (DT) systems**, requiring interoperability across **external systems, enterprise architectures, and other DTs**. Key aspects include:
  1. **Data Exchange Protocols**: While no specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST, AMQP) are mentioned, the integration of **diverse modeling tools** (SysML, Simulink, C++, Java) implies the need for **standardized data serialization formats** (e.g., JSON, XML, Protocol Buffers) and **APIs** to facilitate cross-tool communication. The **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) suggests a **contextual data aggregation layer**, which may rely on **event-driven or publish-subscribe mechanisms** for external data ingestion.
  2. **Interoperability Standards**: The document references **MBSE methodologies** (RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT) and **ADLs** (ACME, AADL, MontiArc), which inherently support **modularity and standardization** but do not specify industry-wide standards like **ISO 23247 (Digital Twin Framework)** or **IEC 62443 (Industrial Automation)**. Integration with **enterprise systems** (e.g., ERP, MES, PLM) would require **middleware solutions** to translate between domain-specific models and DT representations.
  3. **Dependencies on External Systems**: The DT’s ability to function depends on **real-time or near-real-time data feeds** from physical assets, sensors, or legacy systems. The document does not specify **latency tolerances** or **data freshness requirements**, but the **active processing** of digital shadows (vs. passive collection) implies **low-latency dependencies** for closed-loop control or predictive analytics. External systems may also include **third-party DTs or federated architectures**, necessitating **security and trust frameworks** (e.g., blockchain for data provenance, OAuth for authentication).
  4. **Integration Patterns**: The **component & connector perspective** of ADLs (e.g., MontiArc) suggests a **service-oriented or microservices-based integration pattern**, where DT modules expose **well-defined interfaces** for external consumption. The document’s emphasis on **stakeholder integration** further implies **hybrid integration patterns**, combining **ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipelines** for batch data with **streaming architectures** (e.g., Apache Kafka) for real-time updates. No specific mention of **DT federation** or **inter-DT communication protocols** is provided."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through the **safety cell**, which restricts the maximum working range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]** and enforces operational boundaries to prevent collisions or unsafe conditions. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implicitly supported by the **dual encoder system** (motor encoder and secondary encoder behind the gearbox), which mitigates backlash and positional uncertainty by cross-referencing encoder readings to infer elasticity and friction in the drive train. **Cybersecurity protocols** are indirectly addressed via the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**, a specialized system for subtractive manufacturing (e.g., milling) with **access control mechanisms** for axis-specific inputs (position, speed, acceleration) and outputs (target/actual values of position, derivatives, and motor current). The system’s **risk assessment** is embedded in its design, as the **gear transmission and cycloidal gear** ensure torque and speed constraints (e.g., **|q̇_max| = 75°/s**) are met, while the **permanent magnet synchronous motor**’s torque (**τ_M**) and current (**I_M**) are monitored for operational integrity. No explicit mention of **authentication systems**, **threat mitigation strategies**, or **data protection regulations** (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001) is provided in the document."
]
